<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The automatic suffix in basic-seo.html is already committed and working.

Manual "| JoRap Notes" entries will be removed from five content files. Custom suffixes in getting-started.md and graph.md will also be stripped. The template may be enhanced to detect and remove partial suffixes before appending.

Frontmatter titles will contain only the page-specific portion. The template will always append "| JoRap Notes".

The home page title already contains "JoRap Notes" as its prefix, so it needs no changes. Frontmatter titles will be simplified to plain page names or brief descriptive phrases. A dedicated metadata title suffix option is unnecessary because site.Title already resolves correctly.

The template will strip any existing ` | JoRap Notes` suffix before reappending it. This normalizes titles that already contain the suffix. The logic is being simplified.

The template already appends the site title only when no matching suffix exists. Because getting-started.md uses a different suffix, it gets double-suffixed. The chosen approach is to strip any existing ` | *` suffix before reappending the site title. This automatically normalizes titles that already contain a different trailing suffix.

Stripping any pipe suffix would break legitimate titles that contain pipes elsewhere. The safer approach is to target only known site suffixes such as ` | JoRap Notes`, ` | JoRap`, or ` | Notes Garden`. Using `site.Params.logo_text` or `site.Title` as the canonical suffix avoids ambiguous matches.

The template will strip only exact ` | {site.Title}` suffixes. Pipes in titles likely indicate section separators rather than suffixes. The "Getting Started | Notes Garden" example appears to separate a page title from a section label.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

_**Agent (default)**_

This is already handled in your SEO partial — I tightened it up so you never need to add the suffix in frontmatter.

**How it works:** `layouts/_partials/basic-seo.html` (your override of the basic-seo module) appends ` | JoRap Notes` to every non-homepage title. It resolves the page title from `meta_title` → `title` → taxonomy title, strips an existing suffix if present (so manual additions don’t double up), then appends the suffix.

**What changed:**
1. **`config/_default/params.toml`** — added `metadata.title_suffix = "JoRap Notes"` so the suffix is configured in one place
2. **`layouts/_partials/basic-seo.html`** — uses that config and always normalizes before appending
3. **Content cleanup** — removed manual `| JoRap Notes` from About, Contact, Privacy, Elements, and Drafts (and fixed legacy suffixes on Getting Started and Notes Graph)
4. **`static/admin/config.yml`** — CMS hint notes the suffix is added automatically

**Usage:** Set only the page-specific part in frontmatter:

```yaml
meta_title: "About"
```

That renders as `About | JoRap Notes`. Same for pages without `meta_title` — they fall back to `title` and get the suffix.

**Homepage exception:** The homepage uses `meta_title` verbatim (currently the full tagline), since it’s meant to be a custom title rather than `Page | JoRap Notes`.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The graph-data template fails at line 72 with a division-by-zero error.

The expression `mul (sub (len $byDeg) 1) $hubPct | div 100` is being parsed to find the zero divisor.

The default `$hubPct` value is 85, so it is unlikely to be zero. `$hubMinTotal` is not used as a divisor either. The next step is to inspect `graph-data-local.html` and verify the exact character position at line 72.

The `div 100` chain is safe. The zero divisor likely comes from `$hubPct` being nil or zero at runtime despite the default of 85.

Hugo may eagerly evaluate both branches of `cond` before choosing the winner. When `$byDeg` is empty, the false branch computes `-85 / 100`, which is safe and does not trigger division by zero. This rules out the empty list as the cause of the error.

The `div 100` chain is safe even if `$hubPct` were zero. The error likely stems from integer division truncation or a different parse path.

The local graph filter in `graph-data-local.html` may be returning an empty set for this note. If `$byDeg` is empty, the `cond` branch containing the division is skipped entirely, which would explain why the error still occurs.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The build now succeeds locally.

The div-by-zero error likely stems from Hugo 0.163 operator parsing on line 72. The template pipeline may be splitting the expression incorrectly, causing division by zero.

The divisor is 100, so division by zero is impossible unless Hugo splits the pipeline incorrectly. A zero `$hubPct` value is unlikely since the parameter defaults to 85.

Hugo's `cond` function does not short-circuit. It evaluates both branches regardless of the condition. When `$byDeg` is empty, the false branch still runs and triggers the division error.

The false branch in `cond` would yield zero rather than trigger a division error. The real culprit may be `$hubMinTotal` on line 73.

The error is a division failure, not an index out of bounds. The `mul` and `div` operators in Hugo 0.163 may be parsing the pipeline incorrectly, causing the division by zero.

The `div` call may be missing its second argument. If `$hubPct | div` lacks a divisor, Go template math defaults to zero and causes division by zero. I will test this hypothesis in Hugo.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The local build succeeds, so the failure is environment-specific.

The div-by-zero error likely stems from Hugo 0.163 template parsing. The expression `mul ... | div 100` may be evaluated differently than expected, causing division by zero.

Hugo's `mul` function may accept multiple arguments. The pipe operator could therefore bind `div 100` to `mul` as a third argument instead of dividing the result. This would change the expression evaluation and cause the division by zero.

The expression should parse correctly as two arguments to `mul`, then pipe to `div 100`. A zero `$hubPct` value is unlikely since the config sets it to 85. The remaining suspicion is that Hugo may be binding the pipe to `mul` as a third argument instead of dividing the result.

The error message shows 100 as the divisor, not the dividend. This suggests the expression may be evaluating as `div X 0` rather than `div 0 100`. If `$hubPct` is zero, the multiplication yields zero, but the error points to a different parsing path.

The `cond` wrapper confirms `100` is inside the true branch. This rules out `100` being misread as the false branch of another `cond`. The focus returns to whether Hugo binds the pipe to `mul` as a third argument, leaving `div` with only one argument and causing the zero-divide error.

The pipe operator normally sends the piped value as the first argument to `div`, making 100 the divisor. A zero dividend would be harmless. The suspicion now shifts to whether Hugo 0.163 might interpret `div 100` as a command where 100 is the divisor and the piped value is the dividend, causing a division-by-zero error if the piped value is zero. The next step is to verify Hugo's `div` syntax against its documentation.

Hugo documentation confirms `div` uses the piped value as the dividend and 100 as the divisor. The fix will replace the expression with an explicit `if` block and parenthesized division to prevent parsing ambiguity. A separate edge case where all nodes have zero degree also needs handling.</details></think>

for path in sorted(root.rglob('*.md')):
    text = path.read_text()
    if '| JoRap' not in text:
        continue
    new = pat.sub(r'\1\2\4', text)
    if new != text:
        path.write_text(new)
        changed.append(str(path))
for p in changed:
    print(p)
print(f'updated {len(changed)} files')
PY</summary>

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 <<'PY'
import re, pathlib
root = pathlib.Path('content')
pat = re.compile(r'^(meta_title:\s*")(.+?)(\s*\|\s*JoRap(?: Notes)?)(")\s*$', re.M)
changed = []
for path in sorted(root.rglob('*.md')):
    text = path.read_text()
    if '| JoRap' not in text:
        continue
    new = pat.sub(r'\1\2\4', text)
    if new != text:
        path.write_text(new)
        changed.append(str(path))
for p in changed:
    print(p)
print(f'updated {len(changed)} files')
PY
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Two fixes:

### 1. Graph render crash (`div by 0`)
The error on `getting-started.md` came from `graph-data.html` line 72. Hugo’s `cond` evaluates **both** branches, so the percentile math ran even when the graph had no nodes, and the piped `div` expression could hit a divide-by-zero.

Replaced it with an explicit `if gt $nodeCount 0` guard and `div (mul ...) 100` so empty graphs are safe.

### 2. Manual `| JoRap Notes` entries
Your **working tree** content is already clean — no `meta_title` values with `| JoRap` or `| JoRap Notes` remain (except the homepage tagline, which is intentional).

The **last commit** still has manual suffixes on About, Contact, Privacy, etc. Those are fixed locally but not committed yet.

The SEO partial now strips legacy suffixes automatically before appending the canonical one:

- ` | JoRap Notes`
- ` | JoRap`
- ` | Notes Garden`

So even if frontmatter still has an old suffix, the rendered title won’t double up.

**In frontmatter, use only the page-specific part:**

```yaml
meta_title: "About"
```

That renders as `About | JoRap Notes`.

Restart `hugo server` to pick up the graph fix — the getting-started page should load without the render error.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The daily notes archive page may be stale build output from an old file.

The stray text appears in frontmatter on some pages. Git HEAD still contains manual entries despite a clean working tree. A CMS connected to remote data may be the cause. I will inspect all index and utility files, then run a batch cleanup to strip the trailing suffixes from every meta_title field.

The homepage meta_title itself ends with a pipe suffix. Blog posts may show the suffix in the browser while frontmatter stays clean. A regex-based strip in basic-seo could catch any trailing ` | {suffix}` across titles.

A `legacy_title_suffixes` list will be added to params.toml for maintainability. The homepage meta_title intentionally keeps its pipe suffix as branding, so it will be left untouched.

A search confirmed the working tree is clean. The remaining visible suffixes likely come from an unsaved edit or a stale deployed build.

The visible suffix may actually be a redundant site tag appended by the template to titles that already contain "JoRap Notes". Pages like `how-i-built-jorap-notes.md` carry the phrase in their title, so the template adds ` | JoRap Notes` at the end. This produces a duplicated phrase such as "How I Built JoRap Notes: A Developer's Guide to Free Static Site Hosting | JoRap Notes". The user likely sees this trailing suffix as unwanted clutter.

The template will now check whether the base title already contains the title suffix. If it does, the suffix will be omitted entirely. This prevents duplicate phrases like "JoRap Notes" from appearing twice in the browser title.</details></think>

import re, pathlib
patterns = [

    re.compile(r'meta_title:\s*["\'].*\|\s*JoRap["\']', re.I),
]

# Simulate template strip logic
suffixes = ["JoRap Notes", "JoRap", "Notes Garden", "JoRap Notes", "JoRap Notes"]
tests = [
    "About | JoRap Notes",
    "About | JoRap Notes | JoRap Notes",
    "Notes Flashcards | JoRap",
    "Getting Started | Notes Garden",
    "How I Built JoRap Notes: Guide",
]
for base in tests:
    b = base
    for _ in range(5):
        for s in ["JoRap Notes", "JoRap", "Notes Garden"]:

    print(f"{base!r} -> {b!r} | JoRap Notes")
PY</summary>

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 <<'PY'
# Simulate template strip logic
suffixes = ["JoRap Notes", "JoRap", "Notes Garden", "JoRap Notes", "JoRap Notes"]
tests = [
    "About | JoRap Notes",
    "About | JoRap Notes | JoRap Notes",
    "Notes Flashcards | JoRap",
    "Getting Started | Notes Garden",
    "How I Built JoRap Notes: Guide",
]
for base in tests:
    b = base
    for _ in range(5):
        for s in ["JoRap Notes", "JoRap", "Notes Garden"]:
            if b.endswith(f" | {s}"):
                b = b[:-len(f" | {s}")].rstrip()
    print(f"{base!r} -> {b!r} | JoRap Notes")
PY
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Two different places this shows up — worth separating them:

### In frontmatter (what you edit)
Local markdown is already clean. No `meta_title` in `content/` ends with `| JoRap Notes` or `| JoRap`.

Those fixes are **only local** — not committed yet. On **GitHub master** (and in Sveltia CMS if it reads master), these still have manual suffixes:

- `about/_index.md`, `contact/_index.md`, `drafts/_index.md`
- `pages/privacy-policy.md`, `pages/elements.md`
- `notes/cards.md`, `graph.md`, `issues.md`, `review.md`

If CMS still shows `About | JoRap Notes`, it’s reading the old committed version. Commit and push the content changes to fix that.

### In the browser tab (what visitors see)
Every non-homepage **should** show `| JoRap Notes` in the tab — that’s the template appending it from `config/_default/params.toml`. Examples:

- `About` → `About | JoRap Notes`
- `Notes Flashcards` → `Notes Flashcards | JoRap Notes`

That’s expected. It doesn’t mean the suffix belongs in frontmatter.

**Exceptions:**
- **Homepage** uses its own full title: `JoRap Notes | Ideas, execution…`
- **Posts about JoRap Notes** keep the name in the title *and* get the suffix, e.g. `How I Built JoRap Notes: … | JoRap Notes`

### What I added
1. **`layouts/_partials/page-meta-title.html`** — central title logic; strips legacy suffixes (`JoRap Notes`, `JoRap`, `Notes Garden`) in a loop before appending
2. **`scripts/strip-meta-title-suffix.py`** — batch cleanup for imports/CMS saves

Even if frontmatter still has an old suffix, the rendered title won’t double up.

---

**Quick check:** Open `content/english/about/_index.md` locally — it should say `meta_title: "About"`, not `"About | JoRap Notes"`. If your file still has the suffix, reload from disk; if CMS shows it, you’re on master until you push.

Want me to commit these content + template changes?

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: test -f /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/categories/games/index.html && rg -o '<title>[^<]+</title>' /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/categories/games/index.html</summary>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

This was a **stale `public/` directory**, not a template bug.

Hugo’s dev server (`pnpm dev` uses `--buildDrafts`) had already rendered `/categories/games/` from the draft Marvel Snap post. A partial clean of `public/` left that file missing while the server still expected it — hence `renderDeferred: open .../categories/games/index.html: no such file or directory`.

**Fix applied:** removed `public/` and `.hugo_build.lock`, then ran a fresh build with drafts enabled. `public/categories/games/index.html` exists again.

**What you need to do:** stop and restart the dev server:

```bash
# Ctrl+C the running server, then:
pnpm dev
```

If it happens again:

```bash
rm -rf public .hugo_build.lock && pnpm dev
```

Avoid running `hugo --cleanDestinationDir` while `hugo server` is still running — that’s what left `public/` in a broken half-clean state.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A `sets` or `card_sets` array should be added to group flashcards by Set Name during Review.

Review currently groups cards by note tags. The user wants explicit Set Names instead. This could be a note-level array, a per-card field, or site-level configuration.

Note-level `card_sets` is the chosen approach. Spine notes typically contain related cards that should group together. This is simpler to maintain than per-card sets. Per-card overrides may be added later. The Anki export script also needs review for tag support.

The implementation plan covers eight areas. Frontmatter will store `card_sets`. Data collection and JSON output will pass sets through. Review pages will filter by sets instead of tags. Documentation and archetypes will be updated. Spine notes may receive the field automatically.

The field will be named `card_sets` to avoid confusion with Hugo taxonomies. Review filtering will use "All sets" instead of "All tags". Note tags are general taxonomy. Sets are explicit flashcard groupings like "Capture habits" or "Review habits".</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Twelve spine notes have review enabled.

Adding a `card_sets` array to note frontmatter. This propagates through the data pipeline to JSON and the review and cards pages. Filtering logic will shift from tags to sets. The Anki export script and documentation also need updates. The twelve spine notes will receive sensible set assignments.

The twelve spine notes are grouped into six sets: Capture, Workflow, Review, Organization, Writing, and Linking.

The focus is implementing the infrastructure rather than populating every note. A couple of example notes will carry the new field. The six sets are Capture, Organize/Para, Review, Linking, Writing/Evergreen, and Focus/Mental Models.

All twelve spine notes will receive set assignments. Cards can optionally override their parent note’s sets via a per-card `sets` field. The collector template will merge note-level sets with any card-level overrides.

The cards-data template will pass the sets array directly. The review and cards pages will iterate over card.sets instead of tags. JavaScript filters will switch to set-based logic using data-notes-review-set attributes. Deck hashing does not need updating.

Session-level filtering is fine because localStorage tracks state per-deck. The Anki export script currently parses inline YAML lists, so it needs a block-list parser to handle multiline `card_sets` arrays. Hugo handles block lists natively, but the Python script must be enhanced.

A helper function will parse both inline and block YAML list formats in the Anki export script. The spine note assignments are being mapped out, starting with Capture and Workflow groups.

The remaining spine notes are now assigned to the six sets. Documentation and archetype templates need updating. Scripts that generate or format notes must preserve the new field.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Implementing `card_sets` on notes (with optional per-card `sets`), wiring it through collection, Review, Cards, and Anki export.

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path

SETS = {
    "capture": ["Capture"],
    "the-trusted-inbox": ["Capture"],
    "the-collectors-fallacy": ["Capture"],
    "building-a-second-brain": ["Workflow"],
    "weekly-review-checklists": ["Review"],
    "para-method": ["Organization"],
    "evergreen-notes": ["Writing"],
    "atomic-notes": ["Writing"],
    "note-relationships": ["Linking"],
    "associative-linking": ["Linking"],
    "signal-vs-noise": ["Focus"],
    "pareto-principle": ["Focus"],
}

notes_dir = Path("content/english/notes")
for stem, sets in SETS.items():
    path = notes_dir / f"{stem}.md"
    if not path.exists():
        print("missing", path)
        continue
    text = path.read_text()
    if "card_sets:" in text:
        print("skip", stem)
        continue
    block = "\n".join(f'  - "{s}"' for s in sets)
    insert = f"card_sets:\n{block}\n"
    marker = "review: true\n"
    if marker not in text:
        print("no review", stem)
        continue
    text = text.replace(marker, marker + insert, 1)
    path.write_text(text)
    print("updated", stem)
PY</summary>

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path

SETS = {
    "capture": ["Capture"],
    "the-trusted-inbox": ["Capture"],
    "the-collectors-fallacy": ["Capture"],
    "building-a-second-brain": ["Workflow"],
    "weekly-review-checklists": ["Review"],
    "para-method": ["Organization"],
    "evergreen-notes": ["Writing"],
    "atomic-notes": ["Writing"],
    "note-relationships": ["Linking"],
    "associative-linking": ["Linking"],
    "signal-vs-noise": ["Focus"],
    "pareto-principle": ["Focus"],
}

notes_dir = Path("content/english/notes")
for stem, sets in SETS.items():
    path = notes_dir / f"{stem}.md"
    if not path.exists():
        print("missing", path)
        continue
    text = path.read_text()
    if "card_sets:" in text:
        print("skip", stem)
        continue
    block = "\n".join(f'  - "{s}"' for s in sets)
    insert = f"card_sets:\n{block}\n"
    marker = "review: true\n"
    if marker not in text:
        print("no review", stem)
        continue
    text = text.replace(marker, marker + insert, 1)
    path.write_text(text)
    print("updated", stem)
PY
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Flashcards now support **`card_sets`** for grouping in Review and on the Cards page.

### Usage in frontmatter

```yaml
review: true
card_sets:
  - "Capture"
cards:
  - front: "Mid-commute spark. My first move?"
    back: "One trusted inbox. Don't organize yet."
```

Optional per-card override:

```yaml
  - front: "..."
    back: "..."
    sets: ["Capture", "Review"]
```

If `card_sets` is omitted, cards fall back to the note’s `tags` for filtering.

### Sets on the 12 spine notes

| Set | Notes |
|-----|-------|
| Capture | Capture, Trusted Inbox, Collector's Fallacy |
| Workflow | Building a Second Brain |
| Review | Weekly Review Checklists |
| Organization | PARA Method |
| Writing | Evergreen Notes, Atomic Notes |
| Linking | Note Relationships, Associative Linking |
| Focus | Pareto Principle, Signal vs Noise |

### Review & Cards UI

Both pages now filter by **set chips** (Capture, Linking, Review, etc.) instead of note tags. Anki export uses `card_sets` as deck tags when present.

Restart `pnpm dev` if the server is running to pick up the JS changes.

---

_**User**_

add multiple card_sets to the notes

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Checking current `card_sets` on spine notes and assigning multiple sets per note.


_**Agent (default)**_

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && hugo --minify -q 2>&1 | tail -3 && python3 <<'PY'
import re, json
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('public/notes/review/index.html').read_text()
m = re.search(r'class="notes-review-data">(\[.*?\])</script>', html)
if not m:
    print('no json')
else:
    cards = json.loads(m.group(1))
    from collections import Counter
    c = Counter()
    for card in cards:
        for s in card.get('sets', []):
            c[s] += 1
    for s, n in sorted(c.items()):
        print(f'{s}: {n} cards')
PY</summary>

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && hugo --minify -q 2>&1 | tail -3 && python3 <<'PY'
import re, json
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('public/notes/review/index.html').read_text()
m = re.search(r'class="notes-review-data">(\[.*?\])</script>', html)
if not m:
    print('no json')
else:
    cards = json.loads(m.group(1))
    from collections import Counter
    c = Counter()
    for card in cards:
        for s in card.get('sets', []):
            c[s] += 1
    for s, n in sorted(c.items()):
        print(f'{s}: {n} cards')
PY
```
